---
title: "GTA 2026 Docker workflow guide"
author: "Global Tuna Atlas"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  eval = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```

# Purpose

This document gives a clean operational guide for running the **GTA 2026 workflow** in Docker.

The workflow can be launched from:

- an already extracted `GTA_2026` data folder;
- a mounted `.zip` archive;
- a direct archive URL, if the image/run command is allowed to access the internet.

The recommended production logic is:

1. build the Docker image with all R/system dependencies;
2. mount the input data and the `jobs/` output folder at runtime;
3. run only the selected workflow steps through `GTA_STEPS`;
4. keep all outputs outside the container through Docker volumes.

# Build the workflow image

Run this from the root of the `geoflow-tunaatlas` repository.

```bash
cd ~/geoflow-tunaatlas

docker build \
  -f docker/Dockerfile.workflow \
  -t gta-workflow:latest \
  .
```

Optional: rebuild without cache when Docker seems to reuse an old version.

```bash
cd ~/geoflow-tunaatlas

docker build --no-cache \
  -f docker/Dockerfile.workflow \
  -t gta-workflow:latest \
  .
```

Check that the image exists.

```bash
docker images | grep gta
```

# Runtime folders

The examples below use these host folders:

```bash
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_runtime_data
```

Recommended host paths:

| Purpose | Host path | Container path |
|---|---|---|
| Extracted raw GTA data | `/home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA` | `/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026` |
| Runtime data folder | `/home/grasset/gta_runtime_data` | `/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data` |
| Workflow outputs | `/home/grasset/gta_output/jobs` | `/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs` |

The `jobs/` volume is important: without it, outputs created inside the container disappear when `--rm` removes the container.

# Check the extracted data folder before launching

Use this when running from an already extracted directory.

```bash
docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  gta-workflow:latest \
  bash -lc '
    DATA_DIR=/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026
    REQUIRED_FILE=iotc_nominal_catch_firms_level0_2026-04-13.csv

    echo "Content of GTA_2026:"
    ls -lh "$DATA_DIR" | head -40

    echo ""
    echo "Checking required file:"
    test -f "$DATA_DIR/$REQUIRED_FILE" \
      && echo "OK: $DATA_DIR/$REQUIRED_FILE exists" \
      || echo "ERROR: required file not found at expected level"
  '
```

If the check fails, the mounted folder is probably one level too high or one level too low.

# Run pre-harmonisation from an extracted folder

Use this when `all_raw_data_GTA` is already extracted on the host.

```bash
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs

docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  -e GTA_STEPS=rawdata \
  -e GTA_DATA_SOURCE=volume_dir \
  -e GTA_DATA_PATH=/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -e GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV=false \
  gta-workflow:latest
```

This runs the three pre-harmonisation workflows:

- nominal catch;
- georeferenced catch;
- georeferenced effort.

The outputs are written under:

```bash
/home/grasset/gta_output/jobs
```

List the latest job folders:

```bash
find /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs -maxdepth 1 -type d | sort | tail -20
```

# Run pre-harmonisation from a mounted ZIP archive

Use this when the raw data are available as a local `.zip`.

```bash
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_runtime_data
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs

docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA.zip:/data/GTA_2026.zip \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_runtime_data:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  -e GTA_STEPS=rawdata \
  -e GTA_DATA_SOURCE=volume_zip \
  -e GTA_DATA_PATH=/data/GTA_2026.zip \
  -e GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV=false \
  gta-workflow:latest
```

The archive is extracted inside:

```bash
/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026
```

which corresponds to this host folder:

```bash
/home/grasset/gta_runtime_data/GTA_2026
```

# Run Level 0 and Level 1 after pre-harmonisation

## Standard case

Use this when the pre-harmonisation part has already been run and the workflow configuration can find the pre-harmonised outputs through the mounted `jobs/` folder.

This skips `rawdata` and starts directly from the harmonised downstream steps needed for Level 0 and Level 1.

```bash
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs

docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  -e GTA_STEPS=nominal,level0,level1 \
  -e GTA_DATA_SOURCE=volume_dir \
  -e GTA_DATA_PATH=/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -e GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV=false \
  gta-workflow:latest
```

Why `nominal,level0,level1` and not only `level0,level1`?

- `level0` depends on the harmonised nominal catch dataset.
- `level1` depends on the harmonised georeferenced catch inputs.
- If the nominal dataset was already created in a previous run and your JSON files already point to it, you can try only `level0,level1`.

```bash
docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  -e GTA_STEPS=level0,level1 \
  -e GTA_DATA_SOURCE=volume_dir \
  -e GTA_DATA_PATH=/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -e GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV=false \
  gta-workflow:latest
```

## Explicit R call with known pre-harmonisation job paths

Use this version when you want a fully explicit run from R.

Replace the three `jobs/...` paths with the actual job folders produced by the pre-harmonisation run.

```bash
docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  gta-workflow:latest \
  Rscript -e "
    data_dir <- '/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026'

    source('R/launching_workflows/GTA_2026_creation.R')

    run_gta_workflow(
      steps_to_run = c('nominal', 'level0', 'level1'),
      data_source = 'volume_dir',
      data_path = data_dir,
      bootstrap_restore_renv = FALSE,
      existing_paths = list(
        raw_nominal_catch = 'jobs/PASTE_RAW_NOMINAL_JOB_HERE',
        raw_data_georef = 'jobs/PASTE_RAW_GEOREF_CATCH_JOB_HERE',
        raw_data_georef_effort = 'jobs/PASTE_RAW_GEOREF_EFFORT_JOB_HERE'
      )
    )
  "
```

Important note: this explicit `existing_paths` block is useful only if the workflow code or JSON configuration actually uses these paths for downstream workflows. If the JSON files already contain fixed or auto-detected job paths, keep the simpler `GTA_STEPS=nominal,level0,level1` example.

# Run Level 0, Level 1, and Level 2 after pre-harmonisation

```bash
docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  -e GTA_STEPS=nominal,level0,level1,level2 \
  -e GTA_DATA_SOURCE=volume_dir \
  -e GTA_DATA_PATH=/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -e GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV=false \
  gta-workflow:latest
```

# Run invalid-data summaries during pre-harmonisation

```bash
docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  -e GTA_STEPS=rawdata \
  -e GTA_DATA_SOURCE=volume_dir \
  -e GTA_DATA_PATH=/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -e GTA_SUMMARISE_INVALID_RAW=true \
  -e GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV=false \
  gta-workflow:latest
```

# Run with no internet access

This is useful to check that the image does not download code or mapping files during execution.

```bash
docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  -e GTA_STEPS=rawdata \
  -e GTA_DATA_SOURCE=volume_dir \
  -e GTA_DATA_PATH=/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  -e GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV=false \
  gta-workflow:latest
```

If this fails because a mapping file is downloaded from GitHub, move the dependency to the Docker build stage. For example, clone or archive `fdi-mappings` during image build and use a local path through an environment variable such as:

```bash
FDIWG_MAPPINGS_DIR=/opt/fdi-mappings
```

# Quick debugging commands

Check what the container sees:

```bash
docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/Downloads/all_raw_data_GTA:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 \
  gta-workflow:latest \
  bash -lc '
    pwd
    ls -lh /home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026 | head
    test -f /home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data/GTA_2026/iotc_nominal_catch_firms_level0_2026-04-13.csv \
      && echo "Data mount OK" \
      || echo "Data mount ERROR"
  '
```

Check that the image contains the expected workflow script:

```bash
docker run --rm gta-workflow:latest \
  grep -n "run_gta_workflow" R/launching_workflows/GTA_2026_creation.R | head
```

Check the wrapper entrypoint:

```bash
docker run --rm gta-workflow:latest \
  grep -n "source" R/launching_workflows/run_gta_2026_workflow_cli.R
```

# Recommended conventions

Use these values consistently:

| Variable | Recommended value |
|---|---|
| `GTA_STEPS` | `rawdata`, `nominal,level0,level1`, `nominal,level0,level1,level2` |
| `GTA_DATA_SOURCE` | `volume_dir` for extracted data, `volume_zip` for a ZIP archive |
| `GTA_DATA_PATH` | container-side path to the mounted data folder or archive |
| `GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV` | `false` inside the production Docker image |
| `GTA_SUMMARISE_INVALID_RAW` | `true` only when invalid-data summaries are needed |

# Notes for future improvement

The current workflow already supports selected steps through `GTA_STEPS`, and the main R function accepts an `existing_paths` list. A useful future improvement is to make the Docker CLI wrapper read existing job paths from environment variables, for example:

```bash
GTA_RAW_NOMINAL_CATCH=jobs/...
GTA_RAW_DATA_GEOREF=jobs/...
GTA_RAW_DATA_GEOREF_EFFORT=jobs/...
GTA_TUNAATLAS_NOMINAL=jobs/...
GTA_TUNAATLAS_LEVEL0_CATCH=jobs/...
GTA_TUNAATLAS_LEVEL1_CATCH=jobs/...
GTA_TUNAATLAS_LEVEL2_CATCH=jobs/...
```

This would make partial reruns and reporting runs easier to launch from Docker without writing a custom `Rscript -e` command.

# Build Docker images

This section gives the build commands for the two Docker images used in the GTA 2026 workflow.

## Build the workflow image

The workflow image is the main image used to run the data production steps: raw pre-harmonisation, nominal, Level 0, Level 1, and Level 2.

Run this command from the root of the `geoflow-tunaatlas` repository:

```bash
cd ~/geoflow-tunaatlas

docker build \
  -f docker/Dockerfile.workflow \
  -t gta-workflow:latest \
  .
```

If you need to force a full rebuild without Docker cache:

```bash
cd ~/geoflow-tunaatlas

docker build --no-cache \
  -f docker/Dockerfile.workflow \
  -t gta-workflow:latest \
  .
```

After the build, check that the image exists:

```bash
docker images | grep gta-workflow
```

## Build the reporting image

The reporting image is built on top of the workflow image and adds reporting-specific dependencies such as Pandoc/LaTeX tools.

Build `gta-workflow:latest` first, then run:

```bash
cd ~/geoflow-tunaatlas

docker build \
  -f docker/Dockerfile.reporting \
  --build-arg BASE_IMAGE=gta-workflow:latest \
  -t gta-reporting:latest \
  .
```

After the build, check that both images exist:

```bash
docker images | grep gta
```

# Run the reporting image

Use `gta-reporting:latest` when you want to rerun summaries or reports from existing workflow outputs.

## Run summary reports from existing Level 0, Level 1, and Level 2 jobs

Replace the `jobs/...` values with the real job folders produced by the previous workflow run.

```bash
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_runtime_data

docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_runtime_data:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  -e GTA_STEPS=summaries \
  -e GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV=false \
  -e GTA_TUNAATLAS_LEVEL0_CATCH="jobs/PASTE_LEVEL0_JOB_HERE" \
  -e GTA_TUNAATLAS_LEVEL1_CATCH="jobs/PASTE_LEVEL1_JOB_HERE" \
  -e GTA_TUNAATLAS_LEVEL2_CATCH="jobs/PASTE_LEVEL2_JOB_HERE" \
  gta-reporting:latest
```

## Run comparison reports from existing nominal and Level 2 jobs

Replace the `jobs/...` values with the real job folders produced by the previous workflow run.

```bash
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs
mkdir -p /home/grasset/gta_runtime_data

docker run --rm --network none \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_runtime_data:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/data \
  -v /home/grasset/gta_output/jobs:/home/rstudio/geoflow-tunaatlas/jobs \
  -e GTA_STEPS=reports \
  -e GTA_BOOTSTRAP_RESTORE_RENV=false \
  -e GTA_TUNAATLAS_NOMINAL="jobs/PASTE_NOMINAL_JOB_HERE" \
  -e GTA_TUNAATLAS_LEVEL2_CATCH="jobs/PASTE_LEVEL2_JOB_HERE" \
  gta-reporting:latest
```

Important: the two examples above assume that the CLI wrapper reads the `GTA_TUNAATLAS_*` environment variables and passes them to `existing_paths`. If this has not been implemented yet, use the explicit `Rscript -e` pattern shown earlier in this document, or run summaries/reports in the same execution where the upstream job paths are still available.
